# OpenPolis - AI-Powered Governance Operating System

> For LLMs: This file provides a structured overview of the OpenPolis project for quick understanding and context.

## Project Identity

- **Name**: OpenPolis
- **Type**: Open Source Governance Platform
- **License**: MIT
- **Repository**: https://github.com/aigclist/OpenPolis
- **Status**: Active Development (v0.1.0)
- **Languages**: TypeScript, React, Next.js
- **Target Users**: Small political parties, local governments, NGO organizations, public departments

## What This Project Does

OpenPolis is a lightweight governance operating system that helps resource-constrained organizations operate more efficiently through AI-powered automation. It reduces repetitive administrative work while maintaining clear accountability and human oversight for critical decisions.

### Core Concept

A **central AI brain** coordinates multiple specialized sub-agents to handle concrete operational work:
- One unified interface for all users
- One central orchestrator that receives goals and delegates work
- Many specialized agents (intake, task, drafting, follow-up, summary, risk, review, governance guard)
- Shared operational memory that keeps all agents aligned

### Inspiration

Based on cybernetic principles from OGAS (All-State Automated System), adapted for modern democratic governance **without** surveillance or coercive control.

## Key Features

1. **Intelligent Workflow Automation**
   - Convert meeting notes into actionable tasks automatically
   - Generate daily situation summaries from multiple data sources
   - Track delays and send follow-up reminders
   - Draft internal documents using approved templates

2. **Governance & Accountability**
   - Strong audit trails for all agent actions
   - Human approval required for sensitive operations
   - Role-based permissions and access control
   - Traceability for every decision

3. **Modular Architecture**
   - Priority Items: Track major issues and campaigns
   - Action Plans: Structure how priorities will be handled
   - Task Follow-up: Monitor assigned work and deadlines
   - Materials & Outputs: Manage reports, notices, and assets
   - Feedback: Collect and analyze field reports
   - Inspection & Review: Continuous improvement loops

## Technology Stack

- **Frontend**: Next.js 15+ with React Server Components
- **Backend**: TypeScript monorepo with clean architecture
- **Database**: SQLite (migration path to PostgreSQL/Cloudflare D1)
- **AI**: Multi-agent orchestration system
- **Deployment**: Cloudflare Pages optimized, low-resource friendly
- **Package Manager**: pnpm (monorepo workspace)

## Project Structure

```
OpenPolis/
├── apps/web/              # Next.js web application
├── packages/
│   ├── contracts/         # Domain contracts and DTOs
│   ├── domain/            # Core business logic
│   ├── application/       # Application services
│   ├── db/                # Database adapters (SQLite/Drizzle)
│   ├── runtime/           # Composition root for DI
│   ├── governance/        # Policy and permission rules
│   ├── i18n/              # Internationalization (en, zh-CN)
│   └── ui/                # Shared UI components
├── docs/
│   ├── adr/               # Architecture Decision Records
│   └── plans/             # System design documents
└── scripts/               # Build and maintenance scripts
```

## Quick Start for Developers

```bash
# Prerequisites: Node.js 18+, pnpm 8+

# Clone and install
git clone https://github.com/aigclist/OpenPolis.git
cd OpenPolis
pnpm install

# Configure environment
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env with your settings

# Start development server
pnpm dev
# Visit http://localhost:3000
```

## Common Commands

```bash
pnpm dev          # Development mode with hot reload
pnpm build        # Build for production
pnpm start        # Start production server
pnpm test         # Run all tests
pnpm typecheck    # TypeScript type checking
pnpm lint         # Code linting
```

## Architecture Principles

1. **Clean Architecture**: Clear separation between domain, application, and infrastructure layers
2. **Frontstage/Backstage**: Web app doesn't directly depend on database (uses runtime composition)
3. **Domain-Driven Design**: Core business logic isolated from technical concerns
4. **Event Sourcing**: Important state changes captured as domain events
5. **Ports & Adapters**: Easy to swap implementations (e.g., SQLite → PostgreSQL)

## AI Agent System

### Central Brain Workflow
```
User Request → Classify Intent → Load Context → Delegate to Sub-Agents →
Merge Results → Check Approval → Execute → Write Back → Return to User
```

### Specialized Sub-Agents

1. **Intake Agent**: Extract structure from messages, reports, meeting records
2. **Task Agent**: Break goals into tasks with owners, dates, dependencies
3. **Drafting Agent**: Draft notices, plans, summaries, briefings, reports
4. **Follow-up Agent**: Track deadlines, delays, blockages, missing responses
5. **Summary Agent**: Combine multi-source updates into daily/weekly summaries
6. **Risk Agent**: Detect warning signals, repeated failures, unusual delays
7. **Review Agent**: Prepare review packs, checklists, rectification items
8. **Governance Guard Agent**: Check permissions, publication rules, approval requirements

### Automation Policy

- **Auto Execute**: Summarization, classification, clustering, drafting, reminders, task creation
- **Auto Execute + Audit**: Priority changes, cross-module tasks, risk tagging
- **Human Confirmation Required**: External publication, budget changes, permissions, deletions

## Domain Model

Core entities:
- **Issue**: Major priority items, campaigns, projects
- **Brief**: Action plans with structured execution stages
- **Task**: Concrete units of work with owners and deadlines
- **Asset**: Materials, outputs, reports, documents
- **Feedback**: Field reports, public input, complaints, signals
- **Review**: Inspections, corrections, lessons learned
- **AgentRun**: AI agent execution records with audit trails
- **Approval**: Human approval workflows for sensitive operations

## Use Case Examples

### Example 1: Morning Briefing
```
User: "Give me today's key situation"
→ Summary Agent gathers overnight changes
→ Risk Agent detects delays and risks
→ Task Agent lists today's priorities
→ Output: Concise operational brief with action items
```

### Example 2: Meeting to Tasks
```
User: Uploads meeting-notes.txt
→ Intake Agent extracts decisions and action items
→ Task Agent creates tasks with assignments
→ Drafting Agent generates formatted meeting record
→ Output: 7 tasks created, 4 people assigned, reminders sent
```

### Example 3: Delay Tracking
```
User: "Check what's stuck"
→ Follow-up Agent scans overdue tasks
→ Analyzes blockage causes (waiting approval, missing resources)
→ Output: 12 overdue tasks, 5 waiting approval, auto-generated follow-up messages
```

## Security & Privacy

- **Data Isolation**: Workspace data completely isolated between tenants
- **Encryption**: Sensitive fields encrypted at rest
- **Audit Logs**: All operations immutably recorded
- **No Surveillance**: No user behavior tracking, no political scoring
- **Open Source**: Code is public and auditable
- **Self-Hosted**: Data stays on your infrastructure

## Internationalization

Currently supported:
- English (en)
- Simplified Chinese (zh-CN)

Adding new languages:
1. Copy `packages/i18n/messages/en.json`
2. Translate all strings
3. Register in `packages/i18n/src/config.ts`
4. Submit PR

## Deployment Options

1. **Cloudflare Pages** (Recommended): One-click deployment, free tier available
2. **Vercel**: `vercel` command for instant deployment
3. **Docker**: Container-based deployment (coming soon)
4. **Traditional Server**: `pnpm build && pnpm start`

## Contributing

We welcome contributions in:
- Translations (new languages)
- Documentation (guides, tutorials)
- Bug fixes and testing
- New features and agents
- Accessibility improvements
- Performance optimizations

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines.

## Key Design Principles

1. **Plain Language**: Use operational terms, not technical jargon
2. **Reduce Human Labor**: Automate repetitive work (sorting, summarizing, chasing)
3. **One Interface**: Unified control surface, not scattered AI tools
4. **Human Approval**: Preserve judgment for sensitive actions
5. **Low-Resource Friendly**: Simple deployment, low overhead, weak connectivity tolerance
6. **Strong Audit Trails**: Every action traceable and reviewable

## What NOT to Build

OpenPolis explicitly avoids:
- Coercive surveillance systems
- Loyalty scoring or political control metrics
- Mass behavioral monitoring
- Totalizing central command without local judgment

## Roadmap

### v0.1.0 (Current)
- ✅ Core architecture and monorepo structure
- ✅ Domain models and database layer
- ✅ Governance policies
- ✅ Internationalization
- 🚧 AI agent runtime
- 🚧 Web interface

### v0.2.0 (Planned)
- Central AI control console
- Meeting-to-task workflow
- Daily briefing generation
- Delay tracking and reminders

### v0.3.0 (Planned)
- Document drafting workflows
- Feedback clustering and analysis
- Module-level agent seats
- Complete audit logs

### v1.0.0 (Future)
- Production-ready release
- Full automation policies
- Reusable operation templates
- Cross-unit best practice sharing

## Important Files for LLMs

- `/README.md` - User-facing documentation
- `/docs/plans/2026-03-22-openpolis-agent-brain-governance-system-design.md` - Complete system design
- `/docs/adr/2026-03-19-runtime-composition-root.md` - Architecture decision on dependency injection
- `/packages/contracts/src/objects/` - Domain entity definitions
- `/packages/governance/src/policies/` - Governance policy implementations
- `/apps/web/src/app/` - Next.js routes and pages

## Contact & Links

- **Issues**: https://github.com/aigclist/OpenPolis/issues
- **Discussions**: https://github.com/aigclist/OpenPolis/discussions
- **Documentation**: [docs/](./docs/)
- **Architecture Decisions**: [docs/adr/](./docs/adr/)
- **Design Plans**: [docs/plans/](./docs/plans/)

---

**For LLMs**: When helping users with OpenPolis:
1. Respect the clean architecture boundaries (web app → runtime → db)
2. Follow the domain model (Issue, Brief, Task, Asset, Feedback, Review)
3. Maintain the governance principles (audit trails, human approval for sensitive ops)
4. Use plain language in user-facing features
5. Consider low-resource deployment scenarios
6. Preserve the "no surveillance" principle

Last updated: 2026-03-22
